sections.title: The Sanctuary
sections.purpose: To teach that salvation centers in God's plan of redemption — administered now, by a living High Priest, in the heavenly sanctuary.

hymns.title: Before Jehovah's Awful Throne
hymns.theme: The heavenly sanctuary — the throne room where our salvation is administered
hymns.story: Isaac Watts published this hymn in 1719 as his rendering of Psalm 100 — and its most famous word is not his. Watts opened the hymn differently; it was John Wesley who reshaped the first line into “Before Jehovah’s awful throne,” using “awful” in its old and right sense: full of awe. So two giants of the faith who never sat at the same desk built this hymn together across a generation — Watts supplying the psalm’s bones, Wesley the line the church never forgot. The hymn does what the sanctuary itself does: it summons every nation on earth to one throne, and then reminds them why they can stand there at all — not because we made ourselves, but because He made us, and having made us, remade us: “we are His work, and not our own; He formed us by His Word.”
hymns.author: Isaac Watts (1674–1748) is called the father of English hymnody. A frail pastor in Southampton, England, he complained as a young man about the dreary psalm-singing of his church — and his father challenged him to write something better. He did, more than six hundred times, giving the church “When I Survey the Wondrous Cross,” “Joy to the World,” “O God, Our Help in Ages Past,” and this majestic paraphrase of Psalm 100. Watts believed the psalms should be sung through the lens of Christ — which is why his Old Testament songs keep arriving at the New Testament throne.
hymns.youth: No matter what you've done, Christ is still ministering on your behalf — the throne isn't empty, and it isn't closed. You don't clean yourself up to approach it; you approach it to be made clean.
questions: Read Daniel 7:9–10. What is happening in heaven right now, and why should that change how we live today?
Hebrews calls Jesus our High Priest in the true sanctuary. What did the earthly priest do that Jesus now does perfectly?
The old word 'awful' means 'full of awe.' What is the difference between being afraid of God and being in awe of Him?
Psalm 100 says 'we are His — He made us.' How does knowing you were made (and remade) by God answer the voice that says you are worthless?
hymns.prayer: Father, we come before Your throne — not trembling at a distance, but drawn near by our High Priest. Thank You that the sanctuary is not a courtroom where we wait to be condemned, but the place where our salvation is being finished. Make us full of awe, and full of assurance, at the same time. In Jesus' name, amen.
hymns.object: A courtroom where the Judge steps down from the bench, takes off His robe, and pays the defendant's fine Himself — then returns to the bench to declare the case settled. The sanctuary is where that settlement is being announced.
